title: 1F: Session Persistence Client
description: Implement buyer-side session management for multi-turn conversations with sellers. Create sessions via POST /sessions, maintain session_id across requests, handle session expiry (7-day TTL). Enable the buyer agent to maintain context across discovery, pricing, negotiation, and booking steps. Maps to seller's 1F (multi-turn session persistence). Cross-repo dependency: seller-v8c (completed).
notes: Quinn verification: VERIFIED. 122/122 tests pass. All acceptance criteria met. Report at docs/reports/QUINN_VERIFICATION_buyer-1ku_2026-03-08.md
close_reason: Quinn VERIFIED 122/122 tests. Tier 2 auto-close. Branch: feature/session-persistence

title: 3F: Deployment & Operations Guide
description: Production deployment and operations documentation. Covers: environment configuration, scaling the agent hierarchy, monitoring and alerting (deal success rates, latency, budget pacing), log aggregation, secrets management for API keys and seller credentials, health checks, and runbooks for common operational scenarios. Mirrors seller's 3C (seller-ryn).

title: 2D: Creative Management Sub-Agent
description: Add a Level 3 Creative Agent that manages creative assets across campaigns. Handles creative spec validation (IAB standard sizes, VAST/VPAID for video, SIMID for interactive), A/B testing assignment, creative rotation strategies, and compliance checks. Works with execution agent for creative-to-line assignment via OpenDirect Assignments API.

title: 3A: FreeWheel Buyer Cloud Integration
description: Integrate with FreeWheel Buyer Cloud MCP (bcmcp.freewheel.com) for campaign execution, creative management, and reporting. This is the buyer-side counterpart to seller's FreeWheel dual-MCP architecture (seller-dcd chain). Phase 1: campaign binding to deals from Streaming Hub. Phase 2: creative upload and activation. Phase 3: reporting and pacing. Cross-repo dependency: seller-dcd (open, P1 — FreeWheel Ad Server Integration).
notes: BLOCKED BY: seller-dcd (Brian). FreeWheel integration requires seller-side FreeWheel support.

title: Docs: State machine and event bus documentation
description:
Add documentation pages for the buyer's state machine and event bus.

## What to Do
1. Create docs/architecture/state-machine.md — document deal lifecycle states, transitions, guards, audit trail. Include mermaid state diagram.
2. Create docs/architecture/event-bus.md — document event types, payloads, emit/query pattern, API endpoints.
3. Update mkdocs.yml nav with both pages under Architecture.
4. Cross-reference seller docs where relevant.

## Style
Match existing docs tone (see docs/architecture/deal-store.md for reference).

title: Implement buyer order state machine (Option A)
description:
Copy the seller's OrderStateMachine framework into the buyer repo and customize for buyer-specific deal and campaign lifecycles.

## What to Do
1. Copy state machine framework classes from seller (OrderStateMachine, TransitionRule, GuardFn, StateTransition, OrderAuditLog) into buyer src/ad_buyer/models/state_machine.py
2. Define buyer deal lifecycle states: quoted → negotiating → accepted → booking → booked → delivering → completed (plus failed, cancelled, expired)
3. Define buyer campaign/booking lifecycle states mapping to existing ExecutionStatus enum
4. Add linear TV lifecycle states: makegood_pending, partially_canceled
5. Wire into DealStore.update_deal_status() to enforce valid transitions
6. Wire into DealBookingFlow and DSPDealFlow (replace direct enum assignments with machine.transition())
7. Tests: transition enforcement, guard conditions, audit trail, invalid transition rejection

## Source Reference
- Seller implementation: ad_seller_system/src/ad_seller/models/order_state_machine.py (on fix-seller-import-crash branch)
- Pure Pydantic + stdlib, no external deps

title: Rewrite buyer design doc as forward-looking roadmap
1G: Linear TV Buying Support
description: Add a buyer-side Linear TV specialist sub-agent (Level 2) for traditional linear television buying. This is distinct from the existing CTV Agent — linear TV has fundamentally different buying mechanics: daypart-based pricing (CPP and CPM), DMA/local market targeting, scatter vs upfront deal structures, GRP-based audience guarantees, makegood terms, and commercial pod positioning. The Linear TV Agent handles negotiations with linear sellers, understands rate-of-change calculations, and works with Mediaocean Lumina (3D-Phase2) for order management. Maps to seller's 1E (Linear TV Specialist Agent). Cross-repo dependency: seller-yu6 (completed).
notes: Quinn verification: VERIFIED. See docs/reports/QUINN_VERIFICATION_buyer-6io_2026-03-11.md. 1016/1022 tests passed (6 pre-existing failures). 51 new linear TV tests all pass. Backward compatibility confirmed. Live smoke test N/A (seller endpoints not yet available).
close_reason: Quinn VERIFIED. PR #35 merged. 51 new tests, 0 regressions. Scatter-only v1, Option C hybrid, DMA-level.

title: 2E: Innovid & Flashtalking Creative Integration
description: Integrate with Innovid and Flashtalking for CTV and display creative serving and measurement. Innovid: CTV creative decisioning, interactive overlays, household-level attribution. Flashtalking: display/video ad serving, DCO (dynamic creative optimization), cross-device tracking. Both provide VAST wrapper URLs for CTV and JavaScript tags for display. The creative sub-agent (2D) should be able to pull creative assets, generate serving tags, and attach them to deals.

title: 2A: Multi-Seller Deal Orchestration
description: Enable the buyer agent to coordinate deals across multiple seller endpoints simultaneously. Discover sellers via agent registry (1B), compare pricing across sellers, run parallel negotiations, and select optimal deals. The Portfolio Manager (Level 1) should orchestrate cross-seller strategy while Channel Specialists handle individual seller interactions.

title: 1D: Media Kit Discovery Client
description: Implement client for consuming seller media kits. Query GET /media-kit for public packages, use API key auth for authenticated package views with exact pricing. Integrate with UnifiedClient for multi-seller media kit aggregation. Maps to seller's 1H (media kit & public inventory discovery). Cross-repo dependency: seller-47z (completed).
close_reason: Quinn VERIFIED 119/119 tests. Tier 2 auto-close. Branch: feature/media-kit-client

title: 2C: Budget Pacing & Reallocation
description: Real-time budget pacing and cross-channel reallocation within campaigns. The Portfolio Manager monitors spend against plan, reallocates budget between channels (display, CTV, mobile) based on performance signals, and adjusts deal parameters mid-flight via seller change request APIs.

title: Implement buyer event bus (Option A)
description:
Copy the seller's event bus into the buyer repo and customize for buyer-specific event types.

## What to Do
1. Copy InMemoryEventBus and event models from seller (EventBus ABC, InMemoryEventBus, Event model, EventType enum) into buyer src/ad_buyer/events/
2. Define buyer event types: quote.requested, quote.received, deal.booked, deal.cancelled, campaign.created, budget.allocated, booking.submitted, inventory.discovered, negotiation.started/round/concluded, session.created/closed
3. Add emit_event() helper for fail-open event emission
4. Add emit calls to DealBookingFlow and DSPDealFlow at each state transition
5. Add GET /events and GET /events/{event_id} endpoints to buyer API
6. Optional: Add SQLite-backed event persistence via DealStore (new events table)
7. Tests: event emission, subscription, filtering, query endpoints

## Source Reference
- Seller implementation: ad_seller_system/src/ad_seller/events/
- InMemoryEventBus is fully portable (Pydantic + stdlib only)

title: 1C: Tiered Identity Presentation Strategy
description: Implement progressive identity reveal strategy for the buyer agent. The DSP agent should strategically choose what identity to present (seat-only, agency, or full advertiser) based on the deal context and pricing tier benefits. BuyerIdentity already has the fields — this adds the strategy logic and HTTP header serialization. Seller's tiered pricing: PUBLIC (0%), SEAT (5%), AGENCY (10%), ADVERTISER (15% + volume). Cross-repo dependency: seller-aeb (completed).
close_reason: Quinn VERIFIED 128/128 tests. Tier 2 auto-close. Branch: feature/identity-strategy

title: 1B: Agent Registry Discovery Client
description: Implement IAB AAMP agent registry client to discover seller agents. Query the registry at tools.iabtechlab.com/agent-registry to find sellers by capability (inventory type, deal types, protocols). Fetch seller agent cards (.well-known/agent.json) for connection details. Register the buyer agent itself so sellers can verify trust. Maps to seller's 1J (agent registry integration). Cross-repo dependency: seller-5np (completed).
close_reason: Quinn VERIFIED 130/130 tests. Tier 2 auto-close. Branch: feature/registry-discovery

title: Epic: Buyer state machine and event bus
description:
Add formal state machine (transition enforcement, guards, audit) and event bus (immutable event logging, pub/sub) to the buyer agent. Option A approach: copy seller's portable implementations into buyer, customize for buyer-specific states and events. Includes documentation.

## Scope
1. State machine: copy framework from seller, define buyer transition tables, wire into DealStore and flows
2. Event bus: copy InMemoryEventBus from seller, define buyer event types, add emit calls to flows
3. Documentation: add docs pages for both

## Design Reference
- Seller state machine: ad_seller_system/src/ad_seller/models/order_state_machine.py
- Seller event bus: ad_seller_system/src/ad_seller/events/
- Research notes in this session's conversation

title: 1H: IAB Deals API v1.0 Client
description: Implement buyer-side client for the IAB Tech Lab Deal Sync API v1.0. This replaces the DSP sub-agent approach — instead of a DSP-specific agent, the buyer system receives deal pushes from sellers via the standardized IAB Deals API and manages deal lifecycle on the buyer side. Capabilities: receive inbound deal pushes (HTTP POST endpoint), respond with BuyerSeat status (pending/approved/rejected/ready_to_serve/active/complete), parse full IAB Deal objects (terms, inventory, curation), manage deal status transitions, and activate approved deals in DSP platforms (TTD, DV360, Amazon DSP) using the Deal IDs from the API. The DSP Agent (Level 2) should be refactored into a Deal Activation Agent that works with IAB Deals API deals rather than discovering deals independently. Maps to seller's 2D (IAB Deals API v1.0 Integration). Cross-repo dependency: seller-a3k (open, P2).
notes: UNBLOCKED: seller-a3k is complete as of 2026-03-10. All 4 endpoints (POST/GET quotes, POST/GET deals) are live on seller main. Ready to implement.
close_reason: Quinn VERIFIED. DealsClient with 4 seller endpoints, auth, retry, DealStore integration. 291/291 passing. Branch: feature/iab-deals-api-client.

title: 3G: Infrastructure-as-Code Deployment (CloudFormation/Terraform)
description: IaC templates for deploying the buyer agent system to AWS/GCP. Includes: ECS/Fargate or Cloud Run service definitions, Redis for production storage, API Gateway for the REST interface, IAM roles and secrets, VPC configuration, and CI/CD pipeline definitions. Mirrors seller's 3G (seller-299).

title: 3D: Mediaocean Order Management Integration
description: Parent task for Mediaocean OMS integrations. Mediaocean is the dominant buy-side order management platform used by major holding companies. Two sub-systems: Prisma (digital/programmatic) and Lumina (linear TV). Both support TIP standard for electronic order exchange with sellers.

title: 1A: API Key Authentication Client
description: Implement client-side API key management for authenticating with sellers. Store API keys per seller endpoint, send via Authorization: Bearer or X-Api-Key headers. Maps to seller's 1I (buyer agent auth & access tiers). The seller issues ask_live_ prefixed keys that map to BuyerIdentity — the buyer client must store these securely, attach them to outgoing requests, and handle 401 responses (expired/revoked keys) gracefully. Cross-repo dependency: seller-aeb (completed).
close_reason: Quinn VERIFIED 129/129 tests. Tier 2 auto-close. Branch: feature/api-key-auth

title: 3D-Phase1: Mediaocean Prisma (Digital/Programmatic)
description: Integrate with Mediaocean Prisma for digital and programmatic order management. Prisma is the industry-standard buy-side OMS used by major agencies (GroupM, Publicis, IPG, Dentsu) for display, video, and CTV. Integration enables: order plan export from Prisma to buyer agent, automated IO generation, deal ID mapping back to Prisma line items, pacing/delivery data sync, and invoice reconciliation. Supports Prisma API and TIP standard for electronic order exchange.

title: 3B: Order Lifecycle State Machine
description: Implement buyer-side order state tracking mirroring the seller's state machine (draft, pending_approval, approved, delivering, completed, canceled). Handle state transitions, approval gates, and cancellation flows. Maps to seller's 2A (order workflow state machine). Cross-repo dependency: seller-awh (open, P2).
notes: BLOCKED BY: seller-awh (Brian). Order lifecycle requires seller-side order state machine.

title: 1E: Multi-Turn Negotiation Client
description: Implement buyer-side negotiation protocol for multi-turn counter-offers with sellers. Send counter-proposals via POST /proposals/{id}/counter with price, volume, and terms adjustments. Handle NegotiationStrategy responses (COLLABORATIVE, STANDARD, FIRM). Track negotiation history and know when to accept vs counter. Maps to seller's 2B (enhanced negotiation engine). Cross-repo dependency: seller-8hx (completed).
close_reason: Quinn VERIFIED 128/128 tests. Tier 2 auto-close. Swappable strategy pattern implemented. Branch: feature/negotiation-client

title: 2B: Campaign Brief to Deal Pipeline
description: End-to-end automated pipeline from campaign brief JSON to booked deals with Deal IDs. Integrates audience planning (UCP), inventory discovery (media kit), pricing evaluation, negotiation, and booking into a single flow. Extends the existing DealBookingFlow and DSPDealFlow with auth (1A), negotiation (1E), and session (1F) capabilities.

title: 3E: Builder Guides for Vertical Customization
description: Guides for customizing the buyer agent for specific verticals (retail media, pharma, automotive, financial services). Covers: adding custom Level 2 channel specialists, configuring deal type preferences per vertical, custom audience taxonomy mapping, vertical-specific compliance rules (e.g. pharma pre-clearance), and integration patterns for vertical-specific DSPs. Mirrors seller's 3B (seller-nur).

title: 3D-Phase2: Mediaocean Lumina (Linear TV)
description: Integrate with Mediaocean Lumina for linear TV order management. Lumina is the linear TV buying and stewardship platform used by agencies for national and local TV. Integration enables: linear TV buy plan import, daypart/program-level order placement, makegood tracking and resolution, post-log reconciliation, and audience delivery verification (Nielsen/Comscore). The Linear TV buyer agent (1G) should use Lumina as its order management backbone. Supports TIP standard for electronic avails, orders, and invoicing with sellers.

title: 3C: API & SDK Documentation
description: Comprehensive documentation for the buyer system: API reference for REST endpoints, SDK usage guide for UnifiedClient, agent configuration guide, protocol selection guide (MCP vs A2A vs UCP), and deployment instructions.
